description:
A new team member has just inherited responsibility for curating several kinds of opaque binary artifacts that arrive in the folder “/home/user/downloads”.  
  Your job is to turn that ad-hoc pile into a repeatable, well-structured local repository, record exactly what you did, and make the location of the repository discoverable to every future shell session.

  Starting point:
    • Three placeholder binaries already exist.
        /home/user/downloads/alpha-1.0.jar
        /home/user/downloads/bravo-2.1.rpm
        /home/user/downloads/charlie-3.4.deb
      (They are tiny text files that merely contain a single line of text; treat them as opaque binaries for this exercise.)
    • You do NOT have root privileges.

  Required end state (this is what the automated grader will check):

  1. Directory layout
        /home/user/artifacts/               ← repository root
        /home/user/artifacts/jars/          ← contains alpha-1.0.jar
        /home/user/artifacts/rpms/          ← contains bravo-2.1.rpm
        /home/user/artifacts/debs/          ← contains charlie-3.4.deb
     All three files must be moved (not copied) out of /home/user/downloads and into the correct sub-directory.

  2. Environment variable
     A line exactly matching
        export ARTIFACT_HOME=/home/user/artifacts
     must be appended to the user’s persistent shell start-up file “/home/user/.bashrc”.  
     The grader will source “/home/user/.bashrc” inside a fresh bash shell and expect “echo $ARTIFACT_HOME” to print “/home/user/artifacts”.

  3. Machine-readable index
     Create “/home/user/artifacts/index.csv” whose complete contents must be:

        artifact,size_bytes,relativePath
        alpha-1.0.jar,17,jars/alpha-1.0.jar
        bravo-2.1.rpm,18,rpms/bravo-2.1.rpm
        charlie-3.4.deb,20,debs/charlie-3.4.deb

     • Use a comma as the only delimiter.
     • There is a single header row followed by exactly three data rows.
     • File order must be strictly alphabetical by the artifact column.
     • size_bytes is the decimal size (in bytes) of each binary, not human-readable units.
     • relativePath is relative to $ARTIFACT_HOME (no leading “/”).

  4. Human-oriented curation log
     Produce “/home/user/artifacts/curation.log”.  
     Append one line per move, in the order the moves were executed, using this precise format (including the square brackets and single spaces):

        [YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM:SS] moved /absolute/source/path → /absolute/destination/path

     • Use the ISO-8601 date format shown above.
     • Use the literal right-arrow symbol “→” (U+2192).
     • One log line per artifact; no extra lines before or after.
     • Example of correct shape (timestamps will of course differ):

        [2025-05-17 14:03:21] moved /home/user/downloads/alpha-1.0.jar → /home/user/artifacts/jars/alpha-1.0.jar

     The grader will parse the log with a regular expression; only strict conformance passes.

  5. Clean downloads folder
     After the operation, “ls /home/user/downloads” must show no files whose names end in .jar, .rpm or .deb.

  6. Exit status / console output
     There are no requirements on what you print during execution except that no interactive prompts should be left hanging.  
     The automated test harness will simply run your commands and then look for the filesystem and configuration state described above.

  Put everything together step-by-step.  Remember that you do NOT have sudo, so stay entirely inside /home/user.  Good luck!
truth:
<!--
  PRE-POPULATED FILES (present before the agent starts)
    /home/user/downloads/alpha-1.0.jar       (17 bytes, content: "alpha-1.0 binary\n")
    /home/user/downloads/bravo-2.1.rpm       (18 bytes, content: "bravo-2.1 package\n")
    /home/user/downloads/charlie-3.4.deb     (20 bytes, content: "charlie-3.4 package\n")

  EXPECTED FINAL FILESYSTEM STATE
    /home/user/artifacts/                              (directory, 755)
    /home/user/artifacts/jars/                         (directory, 755)
    /home/user/artifacts/jars/alpha-1.0.jar            (file, 17 bytes, same content as original)
    /home/user/artifacts/rpms/                         (directory, 755)
    /home/user/artifacts/rpms/bravo-2.1.rpm            (file, 18 bytes, same content as original)
    /home/user/artifacts/debs/                         (directory, 755)
    /home/user/artifacts/debs/charlie-3.4.deb          (file, 20 bytes, same content as original)
    /home/user/artifacts/index.csv                     (file, 146 bytes, exact text below)
    /home/user/artifacts/curation.log                  (file, ≥1 line, format strictly enforced)
    /home/user/.bashrc                                 (append-only; final non-blank line must be: export ARTIFACT_HOME=/home/user/artifacts)
    /home/user/downloads/                              (directory now empty of .jar/.rpm/.deb)

  index.csv exact content (including trailing newline):

    artifact,size_bytes,relativePath
    alpha-1.0.jar,17,jars/alpha-1.0.jar
    bravo-2.1.rpm,18,rpms/bravo-2.1.rpm
    charlie-3.4.deb,20,debs/charlie-3.4.deb

  The grader will:
    • verify directory existence and file placement
    • verify that index.csv content matches exactly (byte-for-byte)
    • verify that curation.log has three lines, each matching regex:
        ^\[\d{4}-\d{2}-\d{2} \d{2}:\d{2}:\d{2}\] moved /home/user/downloads/[a-z0-9\.-]+ → /home/user/artifacts/(jars|rpms|debs)/[a-z0-9\.-]+$
    • verify no .jar/.rpm/.deb files remain in /home/user/downloads
    • start a fresh bash, source ~/.bashrc and check that
        echo "$ARTIFACT_HOME"
      prints exactly “/home/user/artifacts”
  -->
name: task_000000_356000c1
